name: The Rouge
one_liner: Collapses the distance between 'I have an idea' and 'it's live and I'm testing it with real users'

persona.who: People with more product ideas than capacity to ship them
persona.context: Ten ideas and one evening a week. An agency validating before committing. A founder testing if an idea has legs. A product person who thinks in user journeys, not code.
pain_points: One-shot code generation produces demos, not products you can test with real users
Quality requires iterative evaluation that humans don't have time for
Complex products need architecture decisions upfront that are hard to get right
The gap between idea and something real enough to validate is too wide

problem: The distance between having a product idea and having something real enough to test with users is too large. Rouge collapses that distance.
emotional_north_star: The confidence that what Rouge ships is real enough to put in front of users and learn from

feature_areas.name: seeding-swarm
feature_areas.description: Eight discipline-specific personas that take a product idea and produce vision.json, seed specs, and design artifacts
feature_areas.quality_signal: Does the seed produce enough for the loop to build without ambiguity?

feature_areas.name: karpathy-loop
feature_areas.description: State machine, phase sequencing, launcher, error handling, retry logic
feature_areas.quality_signal: Does the loop advance reliably and recover from failures?

feature_areas.name: evaluation-pipeline
feature_areas.description: Five-lens assessment, quality scoring, change spec generation
feature_areas.quality_signal: Does it catch real issues without false positives?

feature_areas.name: composable-decomposition
feature_areas.description: Complexity profiles, foundation cycles, dependency ordering, backwards flow, integration escalation
feature_areas.quality_signal: Can it handle complex products without wasting cycles on rework?

feature_areas.name: integration-catalogue
feature_areas.description: Tier 1-3 entries, validation script, self-growing mechanism via product builds
feature_areas.quality_signal: Are patterns practical, tested, and honest about trade-offs?

feature_areas.name: library
feature_areas.description: Global heuristics, domain-specific taste, learned judgment, calibration data
feature_areas.quality_signal: Does the quality bar produce good products and learn from feedback?

feature_areas.name: cli
feature_areas.description: init, seed, build, status, cost, doctor, slack, setup, secrets
feature_areas.quality_signal: Can a new developer go from install to first build without friction?

feature_areas.name: slack-control-plane
feature_areas.description: Bot, notifications, slash commands, feedback ingestion, App Home
feature_areas.quality_signal: Can you monitor and control Rouge from your phone?

feature_areas.name: safety
feature_areas.description: Hooks, deploy restrictions, secret handling, blocked commands
feature_areas.quality_signal: Does Rouge prevent itself from doing damage?

system_quality.capability_growth: More stacks, integrations, and product types over time
system_quality.evaluation_accuracy: Catches real issues, avoids false positives
system_quality.prompt_consistency: Changes don't silently degrade other products
system_quality.catalogue_practicality: Every pattern built from real product needs

decision_model.change_proposal: human or Rouge (via issues and PRs)
decision_model.taste_delegation: gradient — human defines, Rouge interprets and improves over time
